spots.name: Chureito Pagoda
spots.trivia: A five-story pagoda framing Mt. Fuji in the distance — one of the most photographed views in all of Japan.

spots.name: Kawaguchiko North Shore
spots.trivia: The north shore of Lake Kawaguchi offers a mirror-like reflection of Mt. Fuji on still winter mornings.

spots.name: Fujisan Hongu Sengen Shrine
spots.trivia: The main shrine of the Sengen network, dedicated to Princess Konohanasakuya-hime, goddess of Mt. Fuji.

spots.name: Fuji 5th Station
spots.trivia: The most accessible point on Mt. Fuji at 2,305m. The starting point for most summit climbers on the Yoshida Trail.

spots.name: Saiko Iyashi-no-Sato
spots.trivia: A reconstructed historic village of traditional thatched farmhouses (kominka) with Fuji views across Lake Saiko.

spots.name: Fujinomiya Yakisoba Street
spots.trivia: Fujinomiya city's famous B-grade gourmet specialty — chewy noodles with lard, dried sardines, and local sauce.

spots.name: Oshino Hakkai Springs
spots.trivia: Eight crystal-clear ponds fed by snowmelt from Mt. Fuji filtering through lava rock over 80 years underground.

spots.name: Tanuki Lake Viewpoint
spots.trivia: A quiet lake on the south side of Fuji offering serene reflections and a less-crowded alternative to Lake Kawaguchi.

spots.name: Fuji Sengen Shrine Yoshida
spots.trivia: The start of the historic Yoshida pilgrimage trail. Stone lanterns line the path leading toward the summit.

spots.name: Aokigahara Forest Trail
spots.trivia: The hushed Sea of Trees at Fuji's base, formed from hardened lava 1,200 years ago. Compasses reportedly malfunction here.

spots.name: Fuji Visitor Centre
spots.trivia: An educational centre at the 5th station explaining Fuji's volcanic history, folklore, and climbing ethics.

spots.name: Shibazakura Festival Ground
spots.trivia: A hillside carpet of pink moss phlox (shibazakura) blooming every spring with Fuji towering behind.

spots.name: Lake Yamanakako
spots.trivia: The largest of the Fuji Five Lakes. On clear days, Fuji's 'Diamond Fuji' crown appears over the water at sunrise.

spots.name: Narusawa Ice Cave
spots.trivia: A natural lava cave at the base of Fuji where ice formations persist year-round due to the constant 0°C temperature.

spots.name: Fujimori Distillery
spots.trivia: A winery in the Fuji foothills producing Shingen wines from local Koshu grapes, with panoramic vineyard Fuji views.

spots.name: Kitano Tenmangu Fujiyoshida
spots.trivia: A shrine honouring the scholar-god Tenjin, draped in moss and old-growth cedar above Fujiyoshida town.

spots.name: Fuji Subaru Line Viewpoint
spots.trivia: A dramatic roadside viewpoint at 2,000m where clouds form below the car while Fuji's summit remains clear above.

spots.name: Gotemba Premium Outlets
spots.trivia: Japan's largest outlet mall, uniquely positioned with a full-face view of Mt. Fuji rising above the shopping promenade.

spots.name: Mishima Skywalk
spots.trivia: Japan's longest pedestrian suspension bridge at 400m. On clear days, Fuji's snow cap is visible directly ahead.

spots.name: Kawaguchiko Music Forest
spots.trivia: A European-inspired museum of mechanical musical instruments set in a lakeside garden with Mt. Fuji as its backdrop.
